Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Abstract explicitly calls CHS 'the enzyme catalyzing the first committed step of the pathway'.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results show HvCHS controls acceptance of O-methylated substrates and affects final homoeriodictyol/hesperetin titers.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The text and assay setup support that HvCHS uses hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA starter units with malonyl-CoA to form products leading to flavanones; in vivo products were homoeriodictyol and hesperetin, and in vitro feruloyl-CoA plus malonyl-CoA were used.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Feruloyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA are directly supported in the in vitro assay. Isoferuloyl-CoA is inferred from feeding isoferulic acid and downstream hesperetin production, but not directly measured or named in the text.

Primary_Product.reason: CHS forms chalcone intermediates that are then converted by CHI to flavanones; this is directly implied by pathway design and CHI inclusion.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: HvCHS was directly linked to production of homoeriodictyol and hesperetin in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper explicitly discusses these compounds as flavonoids / O-methylated flavonoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Methods say extracellular metabolites were quantified from fermentation samples; thus products were measured in culture broth/supernatant.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Abstract and Discussion describe homoeriodictyol and hesperetin as health-promoting and bioactive for treating human diseases.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: HvCHS was heterologously expressed in engineered E. coli and induced with IPTG during fermentation.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: HvCHS function was tested in the reconstructed pathway together with Os4CL and MsCHI in coexpression strains.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: CHS is explicitly the first committed-step enzyme, and the paper's engineering results identify HvCHS as the main determinant of improved methylated flavonoid production.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show wild-type HvCHS supports methylated flavonoid production and that variants, especially Q232P,D234V, improved titers and gave the best performance.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are used in the paper: heterologous reconstruction, mutagenesis, fermentations, HPLC/HPLC-MS, crystal structures, and in vitro kinetics.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods explicitly state 1 mM ferulic acid or isoferulic acid feeding, IPTG induction, cerulenin supplementation, and 32 h fermentation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results/Discussion show hesperetin production remained much lower than homoeriodictyol and OD600 was similar across strains, indicating no obvious growth penalty in the tested set.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly frames HvCHS as a target for further engineering/directed evolution for microbial methylated flavonoid production.

Other_Important_Info.reason: PDB codes 8B32, 8B35, and 8B3C are explicitly given; O-methylated flavonoids impaired soaking; surface loop effects on in vivo performance are described.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are consistent with the Results and Discussion: first committed-step role, influence of A199/surface loop residues, and superior performance plus higher affinity of Q232P,D234V.
